name: Pemmican
recipeInstructions:
1. Run the dry jerky through a food grinder a few times.
2. In a loaf pan, combine the cranberries, raisins, and brown sugar.
3. Blend the mixture until well combined.
4. Melt the suet and stir it into the cranberry-raisin mixture.
5. Allow the suet to cool and solidify.
This approximates the old style pemmican made to preserve meat without refrigeration and to add some vitamin C values.
